rounds.title: Authentication Lockout
rounds.description: The login system is completely locked — fix it before users are permanently blocked
rounds.storyIntro:
⚠️ CRITICAL SYSTEM FAILURE DETECTED.

You rush into NeuroLink's command center. Screens are flashing red. The CEO is pacing. Investors are on a live call.

The AI-powered platform was minutes away from launch — and everything just crashed.

A holographic figure materializes on the main screen — ARIA, NeuroLink's emergency AI assistant.

'Welcome, response team. I'm ARIA. The situation is critical. Our authentication system has been completely locked out. No user can log in. No admin can access the dashboard. The entire identity layer is frozen.'

ARIA's eyes pulse with urgency.

'You need to fix the login system first. Without authentication, nothing else matters. Solve the three security terminals to restore access. You have 5 minutes before the auth tokens expire permanently.'
rounds.storyOutro:
The authentication panel glows green. Login restored.

ARIA's voice returns: 'Authentication is back online. Users can log in again. But don't celebrate yet — our user interface is completely broken. The frontend is rendering garbage. If users can log in but can't see anything... we're still dead in the water. Move to the next sector. Now.'

questions.question: 🔐 Terminal 1 is flashing. ARIA speaks: 'Our login system uses a standard web protocol for secure authentication. What does JWT stand for? Type the full form to restore the auth module.'

🔐 Terminal 2 sparks to life. 'The password database has been compromised. We need to re-secure it. What is the process called when you convert a password into a fixed-length string of characters that cannot be reversed?'
🔐 The final auth terminal blazes crimson. 'Our users need an extra layer of login security beyond just a password. What is this security method called? Two words.'

rounds.title: Interface Meltdown
rounds.description: The user interface is broken — nothing renders correctly
rounds.storyIntro:
You step into NeuroLink's frontend engineering bay. Screens everywhere show broken layouts, missing components, and garbled text.

ARIA appears on a cracked monitor.

'The UI layer is in total meltdown. Our frontend framework is failing. CSS is not loading. Components aren't rendering. Users who just regained login access are seeing a blank white screen.'

She gestures toward three corrupted terminals.

'The interface was built with modern web technologies. You need to understand how they work to fix this. Solve these three terminals to restore the UI. Clock is ticking — 5 minutes.'
rounds.storyOutro:
The screens around you flicker — and then snap into crystal-clear resolution. The UI is back.

ARIA nods approvingly. 'Beautiful. The interface is rendering perfectly. But we've got a bigger problem incoming. Our cloud infrastructure is misconfigured. The servers, storage, and databases that power NeuroLink's AI engine are all pointing to the wrong endpoints. If we don't fix the cloud layer, the platform has no brain. Move to Sector 3.'

questions.question: 🖥️ The first UI terminal boots up. 'Our frontend is built with a popular JavaScript library created by Facebook for building user interfaces with a component-based architecture. Name this library.'
🖥️ Terminal 2 shows corrupted styles. 'Our stylesheet language extends CSS with variables, nesting, and mixins. What is this CSS preprocessor called? It compiles into regular CSS.'
🖥️ The final UI terminal crackles. 'Our layout system uses a CSS model that distributes space along a single axis — row or column. What is this CSS layout model called?'

rounds.title: Cloud Chaos
rounds.description: Cloud services are misconfigured — the platform's brain is offline
rounds.storyIntro:
You descend into NeuroLink's cloud infrastructure vault. Server racks hum with chaotic energy. Warning lights strobe across the ceiling.

ARIA materializes, her form glitching slightly.

'This is bad. Our entire cloud infrastructure is misconfigured. The AI models can't connect to their data. File storage is pointing to deleted buckets. The database is unreachable.'

She steadies herself.

'NeuroLink runs on cloud services. If you understand how cloud computing works — storage, compute, serverless — you can reconfigure the system. Three terminals. Five minutes. The platform's intelligence depends on you.'
rounds.storyOutro:
The server racks hum with renewed purpose. Cloud infrastructure: online.

ARIA reappears, but this time with alarm in her voice.

'The cloud is back — but we've just detected a massive traffic surge heading our way. Thousands of users are hitting the platform simultaneously. Our servers aren't scaled to handle this load. If we don't prepare for the surge, everything we just fixed will crash again under the weight of real users. This is the final sector. Go. NOW.'

questions.question: ☁️ Cloud Terminal 1 activates. 'Our files and AI model weights are stored in a cloud object storage service by AWS. What is the name of this service? Give the full AWS name.'
☁️ Terminal 2 surges with power. 'NeuroLink's API endpoints run on a compute model where we don't manage any servers. The cloud provider runs our code on-demand. What is this computing model called?'
☁️ The final cloud terminal glows dangerously. 'Our AI data is stored in a managed NoSQL database by AWS that provides fast performance at any scale. Name this service.'

rounds.title: Traffic Surge
rounds.description: A massive wave of users is about to hit — scale or collapse
rounds.storyIntro:
Alarms blare. The main dashboard shows a live traffic graph spiking into the red zone. Thousands of connections per second — and climbing.

ARIA appears one final time, her projection flickering under the system strain.

'This is it — the final crisis. A viral social media post just sent 50,000 users to NeuroLink simultaneously. Our servers are buckling. Response times are through the roof. If we don't scale the infrastructure right now, every fix you've made today will be destroyed in a cascade failure.'

Her eyes lock onto you.

'You need to understand load balancing, caching, and scaling. Three terminals. Five minutes. This is the last line of defense. If you fail here... the system goes dark. Permanently.'
rounds.storyOutro:
The traffic graph stabilizes. Response times drop. The platform holds.

Every screen in the command center turns green. NeuroLink is online. Fully operational.

ARIA appears one final time — calm, composed, almost smiling.

'You did it. Authentication restored. Interface rebuilt. Cloud reconfigured. Traffic survived. NeuroLink is live.'

She pauses.

'The investors are watching the dashboard. Users are flooding in. And it's all... working. Perfectly.'

ARIA's form begins to fade.

'Thank you, response team. You saved NeuroLink. Until the next crisis...'

The screens pulse with a soft green glow. Mission complete.

questions.question: 🚨 Surge Terminal 1 flashes red. 'We need to distribute incoming network traffic across multiple servers so no single server is overwhelmed. What is the name of this technique?'
🚨 Terminal 2 overheats. 'To reduce database load, we store frequently accessed data in a temporary high-speed storage layer. What is this technique called?'

🚨 The FINAL terminal — the last barrier. 'Our system needs to automatically add more server instances when traffic increases and remove them when it decreases. What is this AWS feature called? Two words.'
